# sensors-detect version 3.6.0
# System: Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700K [DG41 Gaming Pro] (laptop)
# Board: Gigabyte Technology Co., Ltd. B550 AORUS ELITE V2
# Kernel: 6.8.0-generic x86_64
# Processor: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X (25/33/0)

This program will help you determine which kernel modules you need
to load to use lm_sensors most effectively. It is generally safe
and recommended to accept the default answers to all questions,
unless you know what you're doing.

Now follows a summary of the probes I have just done.
Just press ENTER to continue:

Driver `coretemp':
  * Chip `Intel digital thermal sensor' (confidence: 9)

Driver `nct6775':
  * ISA bus, address 0x290
    Chip `Nuvoton NCT6779D Super IO Sensors' (confidence: 9)

Do you want to overwrite /etc/modules-load.d/lm-sensors.conf? (yes/NO): y

To load everything that is needed, add this to /etc/modules-load.d/lm-sensors.conf:

#----cut here----
# Chip drivers
coretemp
nct6775
#----cut here----

If you have some drivers built into your kernel, the list above will
contain too many modules. Skip the appropriate ones!

Successful!
